what_it_does: This file defines a product concept called the Live Intent Resolution Surface: a universal, schema-driven AI system that starts with vague human intent and progressively turns it into a structured, executable specification for producing artifacts.
observations: Defines a working name, alternatives, one-sentence definition, and short description.
States a core thesis that most AI workflows assume a ready specification and proposes a pre-specification workflow.
Treats every possible output as a typed artifact with properties, dependencies, constraints, decisions, relationships, success criteria, and executable generation paths.
Introduces Progressive Type Resolution from broad thing to narrow artifact type using accumulated evidence traces.
Replaces confidence scores with tags, counts, structured observations, rejections, and decisions.
Represents each user statement at multiple layers: surface, tag, observation, schema, embedding, graph, and execution.
Describes a non-linear, dynamic, generative, schema-rendered, stateful user experience rather than a chatbot or form.
Outlines a workflow lifecycle from zero-state entry through anchor extraction, possibility generation, reaction capture, schema activation, decision closure, spec compilation, and execution handoff.
Proposes a fan-out/fan-in architecture with many specialized local workers and an arbiter.
Assumes a compute runtime with 32 concurrent local processes, a fast local 4B model, up to 6 larger models, graph/vector/relational/document stores, and a queue/workflow engine.
Specifies data stores: event store, graph database, vector database, document store, relational database, and queue/workflow engine.
Defines core state objects, user model, project model, memory principles, decision closure, execution readiness, and downstream handoff package.
Provides an MVP scope focused on four artifact families and a phased build roadmap with technical modules.
Includes design principles and a key terminology section.
standouts: Universal artifact abstraction applied to any producible thing.
Explicit rejection of confidence scores in favor of traceable evidence tags, counts, and observations.
Live generative surface where the whole UI becomes the conversation.
Fan-out/fan-in multi-worker architecture plus an arbiter.
Workflow-before-the-workflow inversion: helping the specification emerge before normal AI execution.
conventions: Uses hierarchical numbered sections with Markdown headings.
Defines terms in a dedicated terminology section.
Alternates between conceptual principle, rationale, concrete example, and implication.
Treats rejected options as first-class data.
Uses structured text blocks and pseudo-code examples to illustrate state and UI.
concerns: Scope is very large and implementation boundaries are not tightly constrained.
Assumes substantial local compute and multi-model orchestration without concrete cost or latency analysis.
Concepts such as tags, observations, decisions, and assumptions overlap and could blur responsibilities.
No empirical validation of the universal artifact claim across all listed domains.
Risk of over-generating UI surfaces and overwhelming users.
notes: A dense product-design manifesto for a pre-specification AI surface that turns vague intent into buildable specs through tags, schemas, and a live generative UI.
questions: What is the exact schema format and how are schemas authored or extended?
How does the arbiter resolve conflicts among fan-out workers?
What determines whether a decision is blocking or non-blocking?
How are user memory privacy, retention, and ownership governed?
What is the precise handoff contract with downstream workflows?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to define a universal pre-specification system; the file does exactly that, mixing vision, architecture, user experience, and roadmap.
apparent_themes: Evidence-trace reasoning instead of probabilistic confidence.
Reaction and rejection as valid input.
Progressive narrowing from latent intent to executable artifact.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a foundational design brief for a new category of AI product: a structured yet conversational pre-build surface that makes vague ideas executable without forcing users to write requirements.
novelty: The explicit inversion from 'given a spec, produce output' to 'given a person without a spec, help the spec emerge', and the systematic use of tags, counts, and observations as traceable evidence instead of confidence scores.
contribution: It contributes a comprehensive conceptual vocabulary, lifecycle, and architectural sketch for a pre-specification intent-resolution system, including artifact typing, progressive type resolution, schema-rendered UI, decision and assumption visibility, and a fan-out/fan-in worker design.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a product-base design document describing the Live Intent Resolution Surface, a universal schema-driven AI system that elicits vague human intent through a live generative interface and progressively compiles it into an executable specification for any typed artifact.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to define and communicate the core concept, user experience, system architecture, data model, and MVP roadmap for a pre-specification AI surface.
